preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: Startups seeking an intent-aware, ai-first design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes what MODE is — an intent-aware, AI-first design system — and immediately signals relevance for startups. Sets the narrative frame so Validator visitors can quickly assess whether this is worth their time.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: Before a Validator will engage with proof, they need to understand what's actually being offered. This beat outlines core capabilities (intent-awareness, AI-first components, adaptive UX) and frames the outcomes startups care about: faster shipping, higher conversion, less design debt.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators require trust signals before they'll internalize claims. Placing credibility after value lets social proof — customer logos, testimonials from startup founders, partner badges — validate the promises just made, reducing skepticism.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: The Validator archetype demands concrete proof. This beat delivers hard metrics (e.g., conversion lift percentages, time-to-ship reductions, case study snippets) that substantiate the value claims and credibility signals, satisfying their need for data-backed validation.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: At the awareness funnel stage this is a soft CTA — e.g., 'Start a free trial,' 'Explore the docs,' or 'Join the beta.' Positioned after evidence so the Validator feels sufficiently convinced to take a low-commitment next step. No Decision beat is included per awareness-stage rules.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Provides secondary engagement paths — documentation links, community, newsletter signup — for visitors not yet ready to convert. Captures exit intent and ensures no visitor leaves without an alternative route back into the funnel.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes what MODE is — an intent-aware, AI-first design system — and immediately signals relevance for startups. Sets the narrative frame so Validator visitors can quickly assess whether this is worth their time.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validators require trust signals before they'll internalize claims. Placing credibility after value lets social proof — customer logos, testimonials from startup founders, partner badges — validate the promises just made, reducing skepticism.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: full variant is appropriate for awareness-stage pages where exploration and context-setting are priorities. The notes specify 'full for exploration and top-funnel pages,' which aligns with the awareness funnel stage.

Editorial variant is the prescribed choice for awareness funnel stage: it is 'the high-expression awareness layout: centered, full-width, dominant headline, eyebrow context label — no media.' This directly matches the selection rule for awareness funnel stage.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: MODE resolves component behavior, visual emphasis, and copy register to the right experience for every user — without manual variants or QA overhead.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. Runs in your codebase — no vendor dependency.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires variants containing social-proof elements. The 'quote-with-logos' variant aligns with the note that 'Validator variant leads with quote, logos below,' establishing credibility through trust signals appropriate for this archetype.

slots.headline: Trusted by teams that move in code
slots.quote: We were manually maintaining three variants of every landing section. MODE collapsed that into a single source of truth. The resolution engine makes the call — we just ship.
slots.attribution: Founder, early-stage SaaS — MODE Kit customer

page.reasoning: Standard variant is the default for awareness-stage pages. The with-cta variant is reserved for 'key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows,' which is not the current context. Standard footer maintains brand presence without conversion pressure.
